site.name: SinCity
site.eyebrow: Neon grit. Serious stories. Loud city energy.
site.heroDescription: SinCity by Enact is a full-throttle GTA RP world built for cinematic police work, criminal arcs, creator moments, and civilian chaos with just enough style to make every traffic stop feel like a trailer scene.
site.quickPitch: The skyline is humming, the imports are polished, and somebody definitely just filed paperwork for a nightclub they should not be trusted with.

sceneOptions: The skyline is humming, the imports are polished, and somebody definitely just filed paperwork for a nightclub they should not be trusted with.
Dispatch is busy, the casino valet is stressed, and somewhere downtown a perfectly legal business meeting is being held with way too many lookouts.
Half the city is trying to look respectable, the other half is trying not to get searched, and somehow both groups are stuck in the same traffic jam.
A civilian market is drawing influencers, off-duty officers, hustlers, and one person who absolutely did not understand the parking signs.

metrics.label: Peak Queue
metrics.note: Rush hour priority starts to matter fast.

metrics.label: Custom Systems
metrics.note: Economy, factions, dispatch, property, imports, events.

metrics.label: Story Density
metrics.note: Measured in suspicious alley meetings per hour.

quickLinks.description: Start your character concept, save a draft locally, and finish in one pass.

quickLinks.title: Visual gallery

incidents.title: PD pursuit through Pillbox
incidents.details: A stolen Komoda just bounced off two medians, one fruit cart, and the patience of every officer on duty.

incidents.title: Night market overflow
incidents.details: Civilians packed Legion Square for a late market event and somebody tried to sell suspiciously glowing lemonade.

incidents.title: Casino valet dispute
incidents.details: A high-end import was taken without permission, which is technically grand theft and emotionally very rude.

incidents.title: Warehouse alarm trip
incidents.details: South docks sensors tripped during a power flutter, so naturally everyone assumed it was a heist.

bulletins.label: Economy
bulletins.headline: New business registration wave
bulletins.title: City economy is running hot but stable
bulletins.summary: Local businesses are expanding, imports are moving, and the city feels busy without descending into total spreadsheet misery. Good time to push legal arcs and storefront stories.

bulletins.label: Factions
bulletins.headline: Street heat and organized response
bulletins.title: Faction pressure is climbing across the south side
bulletins.summary: Territory talk is getting louder, police surveillance is heavier, and the next reckless meeting in a parking structure is likely to go very badly.

bulletins.label: Events
bulletins.headline: Tonight's social calendar is loaded
bulletins.title: Community events are carrying the softer side of the city
bulletins.summary: Car meets, markets, music nights, and casino gatherings keep civilians active between bigger law and criminal beats, which makes the whole place feel more like a world than a mechanic list.

rhythmCards.title: Priority timezone
rhythmCards.description: Built to feel active for both evening UK players and overlapping North America sessions.

rhythmCards.title: Server tone
rhythmCards.description: Stylish presentation, grounded gameplay loop, and enough bite that people remember their scenes.

rhythmCards.title: Visual flavor
rhythmCards.description: Strong green identity, layered motion, and a little drama without turning into pure gimmick.

zones.title: Law and Order
zones.description: Dispatch pressure, clean comms, proper reports, and pursuits that escalate from routine to cinematic in two intersections flat.

zones.title: Civilian Hustle
zones.description: Businesses, odd jobs, public events, and just enough bureaucracy to make a nightclub opening feel suspiciously official.

zones.title: Criminal Arc
zones.description: Heat management, faction pride, careful escalation, and conversations in parking garages that definitely are not about legal commerce.

zones.title: Creator Fuel
zones.description: Good-looking branding, event hooks, and enough site motion that clips, photos, and server promos have material to work with.

gallery.title: Brand Key Art
gallery.description: Wide-format SinCity identity piece for hero sections, splash screens, and launch promos.

gallery.title: Dashboard Moodboard
gallery.description: The reference dashboard energy translated into a more usable site structure.

gallery.title: Faction Emblem
gallery.description: Pure iconography for loading screens, server cards, and branding detail.

gallery.title: Neon Detail
gallery.description: A tighter crop that works well for social previews and event cards.

gallery.title: Control Room View
gallery.description: Useful as a reference panel for rules, events, tiers, and application funnels.

tiers.name: Bronze
tiers.label: Bronze Supporter
tiers.description: Queue assistance, a clean cosmetic boost, and enough server vanity to start feeling mildly important.

tiers.name: Gold
tiers.label: Gold Supporter
tiers.description: A big step up for regulars who want faster access, louder status, and better import leverage.

tiers.name: Platinum
tiers.label: Platinum Supporter
tiers.description: The flashy lane for players who hate queues and enjoy seeing their name show up in all the right places.

faq.question: Is this a real working page or just a design?
faq.answer: It is a working local app with a live JSON API, server-side persistence for whitelist applications, and a content admin page.

faq.question: Can the whitelist form be connected to Discord or a backend later?
faq.answer: Yes. The submission shape is already structured cleanly enough to post into Discord webhooks, Supabase, Firebase, or a custom backend.

faq.question: Does it work on mobile too?
faq.answer: Yes. The layout collapses cleanly to single-column sections on smaller screens, and the dashboard, form, gallery, and store remain usable.
